text.title: GraphQL API Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every GraphQL API Design application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Every mutation returns a typed `<MutationName>Payload` with at minimum `<entity>` (the result) and `userErrors: [UserError!]!`. Scalar returns (boolean, ID) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Payload type lets you add fields later without breaking; scalar returns paint you into a corner.

rule.statement: Validation and business-rule failures are reported in the userErrors array; only system errors throw GraphQL errors. UserError has at least `field` + `message` + optional `code`.
rule.rationale: Mixing 'user errors' into GraphQL errors makes clients write two parallel error paths; the array gives a single shape.

rule.statement: Authorization is declared via SDL directives (e.g. `@auth(scope: ADMIN)`) on fields and mutations. Resolvers do not inline authorization checks.
rule.rationale: Inline auth checks drift across resolvers; directives keep the policy in one place and auditable from the schema alone.

rule.statement: Paginated lists return `<Type>Connection { edges { node, cursor }, pageInfo }`; mutation inputs are `<MutationName>Input`; payloads are `<MutationName>Payload`. No deviations.
rule.rationale: Predictable naming makes client code generators and downstream tooling work without per-schema patches.

rule.statement: Relations use `id: ID!` (Relay opaque IDs), not raw integer/uuid scalars. Where ownership matters, IDs are namespaced (gid://app/Type/123).
rule.rationale: Raw scalars leak storage detail; opaque IDs let you migrate storage without breaking clients.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
